title: Midnight Creamy British Pork Belly Bake
description: A rich, creamy and spicy British-inspired dish featuring pork belly slices, served in mason jars for a satisfying midnight craving.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and arrange the pork belly slices in a single layer.
Drizzle the coconut oil over the pork belly and rub it in with your hands to coat evenly.
Bake the pork belly in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until it starts to brown and crisp up.
While the pork belly is baking, heat a small saucepan over medium heat and add the tadka spice mix. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes, until the spice mix is fragrant.
Add the onion and garlic to the saucepan and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are softened and lightly browned.
Add the canned diced tomatoes, coconut milk, honey (if using), apple cider vinegar, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-15 minutes, until it has thickened and reduced slightly.
Remove the pork belly from the oven and carefully transfer it to the mason jars or ramekins.
Pour the creamy sauce over the pork belly and serve immediately.
Garnish with fresh herbs or chives, if desired.
Note: You can also make this recipe in a slow cooker. Simply brown the pork belly and cook the sauce in a skillet, then transfer everything to the slow cooker and cook on low for 2-3 hours.
